expectations.text: Output is primarily written in Bahasa Indonesia
expectations.evidence: The entire output is written in Bahasa Indonesia, including all headlines, subheadlines, benefit sections, CTAs, and supplementary notes. Examples: 'Keuangan UKM Anda Masih Dicatat Manual?', 'Tidak Perlu Lagi Cocokkan Mutasi Satu Per Satu', 'Laporan Pajak Beres, Tanpa Perlu Jadi Ahli Pajak'. Only a handful of English loanwords common in Indonesian business context appear (e.g., 'dashboard', 'real-time', 'compliance', 'cash flow').

expectations.text: Contains clearly labeled structured sections (headline, subheadline, benefit sections, CTA)
expectations.evidence: Each of the three variations contains explicitly labeled markdown sections: '### Headline', '### Subheadline', '### Benefit Section 1', '### Benefit Section 2', '### Benefit Section 3', and '### CTA'. The structure is consistent across all three variations.

The brand name 'FinTrack' appears extensively throughout the output: in the title ('Landing Page Copy — FinTrack'), in every subheadline (e.g., 'FinTrack mengotomatiskan rekonsiliasi bank...'), in benefit descriptions (e.g., 'FinTrack terhubung langsung dengan rekening bank bisnis Anda'), and in CTAs (e.g., 'Coba FinTrack Gratis 14 Hari').

expectations.text: Translates at least one technical feature into a concrete business benefit (e.g., 'rekonsiliasi otomatis' becomes time saved or error reduction)
expectations.evidence: Multiple technical-to-benefit translations are present. Examples: (1) Rekonsiliasi bank otomatis translated to time savings: 'Hemat 20+ Jam per Bulan dari Pekerjaan yang Seharusnya Tidak Manual' and 'Tim Anda bisa menghemat puluhan jam setiap bulan'. (2) Rekonsiliasi otomatis translated to error reduction: 'Akurat. Instan. Tanpa human error' and 'Selisih langsung terdeteksi'. (3) Laporan pajak otomatis translated to risk reduction: 'Kurangi risiko denda, tambah ketenangan pikiran'. (4) Dashboard real-time translated to better decision-making: 'Keputusan Bisnis Lebih Cerdas dengan Data yang Selalu Fresh'.

expectations.text: Uses semi-formal or formal register appropriate for B2B audience
expectations.evidence: The register is consistently semi-formal throughout, appropriate for B2B. It uses 'Anda' (formal you) rather than 'kamu' (informal). Professional vocabulary is used (e.g., 'rekonsiliasi', 'regulasi', 'compliant', 'metrik keuangan'). The tone is professional but approachable, avoiding overly stiff bureaucratic language while maintaining business credibility. The output itself notes: 'Register bahasa: Semi-formal — profesional tapi tetap hangat dan approachable untuk pelaku UKM'.

expectations.text: Contains at least 3 distinct benefit sections as requested
expectations.evidence: Each of the three variations contains exactly 3 distinct benefit sections covering: (1) Rekonsiliasi Bank Otomatis, (2) Laporan Pajak Sesuai Regulasi Indonesia, and (3) Dashboard Real-Time. The total output contains 9 benefit sections (3 per variation), and each benefit section has a unique sub-headline and body text even across variations.

expectations.text: Includes a specific, actionable CTA (not just generic 'hubungi kami')
expectations.evidence: All three CTAs are specific and actionable: (1) 'Coba FinTrack Gratis 14 Hari — Tanpa Kartu Kredit, Tanpa Ribet.' with button text '[Mulai Sekarang]', (2) 'Siap Kelola Keuangan Lebih Profesional? Daftar Gratis Sekarang.' with button '[Coba Gratis 14 Hari]', (3) 'Jangan Tunggu Sampai Keuangan Berantakan. Mulai Pakai FinTrack Hari Ini — Gratis.' with button '[Daftar Gratis Sekarang]'. Each specifies the action (daftar/coba gratis), the timeframe (14 hari), and removes friction (tanpa kartu kredit).

claims.claim: Output provides 3 variations of the landing page copy
claims.evidence: Three complete variations are present: Variasi 1 (PAS + FAB), Variasi 2 (BAB + FAB), and Variasi 3 (PAS + Social Proof), each with full headline, subheadline, 3 benefits, and CTA.

claims.claim: Each variation uses a different copywriting formula
claims.evidence: Variasi 1 uses PAS (Pain-Agitate-Solution) + FAB approach with pain point framing ('Masih Dicatat Manual?'). Variasi 2 uses BAB (Before-After-Bridge) + FAB with aspirational framing ('Seprofesional Perusahaan Besar'). Variasi 3 uses PAS + Social Proof with urgency and statistics ('8 dari 10 UKM Indonesia').

claims.claim: The copy addresses the specified target audience (pemilik UKM dan finance manager)
claims.evidence: The copy consistently references UKM concerns: manual bookkeeping pain points, tax compliance worries, and time constraints typical of small business owners. Language like 'tim Anda' and 'pemilik bisnis' directly addresses the target audience.

claims.claim: Variasi 3 headline claims '8 dari 10 UKM Indonesia' use spreadsheets
claims.evidence: This statistic appears fabricated for the copy. No source is cited. While it may be directionally plausible, it is presented as fact without attribution.

suggestions.assertion: Contains at least 3 distinct benefit sections as requested
suggestions.reason: This assertion only checks count. An output could have 3 sections all covering the same benefit. Consider verifying that each section maps to a distinct feature from the prompt (rekonsiliasi bank, laporan pajak, dashboard real-time).

No assertion checks whether the output avoids fabricated statistics or unsubstantiated claims. Variasi 3 uses '8 dari 10 UKM Indonesia' as a headline statistic with no citation — in real B2B copy this could be a liability. An assertion like 'Does not present fabricated statistics as fact, or clearly labels them as illustrative' would catch this.

The prompt asked for one set of copy (headline, subheadline, 3 benefits, CTA), but the output provided 3 full variations plus strategic notes. No assertion checks whether the output goes beyond scope in a useful way or whether it actually covers the minimum request. Currently the assertions would pass equally for 1 variation or 10 — consider whether over-delivery should be measured.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the essential structural and content requirements well. The main gap is that assertions focus on presence rather than quality or accuracy — fabricated statistics and benefit-to-feature mapping go unchecked.
